SUMMARY:
Thanks for the updates, @doumdoum. I've left a few comments for your consideration. The build is also failing at the moment. Please build your changes locally and make sure that the build is successful.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/SpelTagValueExpressionResolver.java:30] Introducing a cache could be worthwhile here, but it should be considered separately. Please align this class's implementation with that of the SpelTagValueExpressionResolver inner class that it replaces.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/SpelTagValueExpressionResolver.java:28] Please add some javadoc.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/metrics/SpelTagValueExpressionResolverTest.java:34] This class should be named `SpelTagValueExpressionResolverTests`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/SpelTagValueExpressionResolver.java:17] Please move this to the metrics package.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/metrics/SpelTagValueExpressionResolver.java:35] `4.0.0`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/metrics/SpelTagValueExpressionResolverTests.java:51] This is largely testing the underlying SpEL support. It could be simplified to test a single valid expression.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/metrics/SpelTagValueExpressionResolverTests.java:36] This is largely testing the underlying SpEL support. It could be simplified to test a single invalid expression.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/metrics/SpelTagValueExpressionResolver.java:39] As discussed, this cache should be considered separately. Please remove.

SUMMARY:
Thanks very much for the PR, @HuitaePark. I've left some comments for your consideration.

- [module/spring-boot-restclient-test/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/restclient/test/autoconfigure/MockRestServiceServerAutoConfiguration.java:62] This same change should be applied to the two other `@Bean` methods in the class.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @dmiska25. I've left some comments for your consideration.

POINTS:
- [module/spring-boot-liquibase/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/liquibase/autoconfigure/LiquibaseAutoConfiguration.java:194] Rather than using a `BFPP`, I think this could be done in the `@Bean` method that creates the `SpringLiquibase` instance. This will ensure it only applies when auto-configuring Liquibase and will back off when the user wants to exert more control.
- [module/spring-boot-liquibase/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/liquibase/autoconfigure/LiquibaseAutoConfiguration.java:197] We don't use `var` in the Spring Boot codebase.
- [module/spring-boot-liquibase/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/liquibase/autoconfigure/LiquibaseAutoConfiguration.java:205] Hopefully this won't be necessary when using a `@Bean` method. If it is necessary, one scenario where the `@Bean` method may be called multiple times is in tests with slightly different configuration that result in the creation of multiple contexts. Removing one provider in favour of a new one may cause problems there.
- [module/spring-boot-liquibase/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/liquibase/autoconfigure/EnvironmentConfigurationValueProviderTests.java:36] Please add some class javadoc. See other `…Tests` classes for examples.
- [module/spring-boot-liquibase/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/liquibase/autoconfigure/EnvironmentConfigurationValueProvider.java:72] I don't think these comments are necessary.

POINTS:

- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-tools/spring-boot-buildpack-platform/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/buildpack/platform/docker/transport/HttpClientTransport.java:167] `StringUtils` is already imported so there's no need to fully-qualify the type.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-tools/spring-boot-buildpack-platform/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/buildpack/platform/docker/transport/ProxyAuthenticationExceptionTests.java:23] I don't think we need tests for `ProxyAuthenticationException`. Please remove this class.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR. I've left a couple of comments for your consideration.

POINTS:
- [module/spring-boot-flyway/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/flyway/autoconfigure/FlywayAutoConfiguration.java:323] Removing this without also changing the property's default value will result in a change in behavior.

`FlywayProperties` should be updated to change the property's default to align it with Flyway and a test added that verifies Boot's default aligns with Flyway's.
- [module/spring-boot-flyway/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/flyway/autoconfigure/FlywayAutoConfigurationTests.java:910] I think this test will pass even without this line due to the problem described above.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironment.java:43] This could be a breaking change as `POSTGRESQL_USER` is [one of the currently supported environment variable names](https://github.com/bitnami/containers/blob/2eb78faa61c13d192cbc563313eabe39d6403443/bitnami/postgresql/17/debian-12/rootfs/opt/bitnami/scripts/postgresql-env.sh#L243-L246):

- `POSTGRESQL_USERNAME`
- `POSTGRES_USERNAME`
- `POSTGRES_USER`
- `POSTGRESQL_USER`

The README [documents `POSTGRESQL_USERNAME`](https://github.com/bitnami/containers/blob/2eb78faa61c13d192cbc563313eabe39d6403443/bitnami/postgresql/README.md?plain=1#L200) so we need to support that. We should also keep support for `POSTGRESQL_USER` to avoid breaking anyone using it.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironmentTests.java:66] This test should be kept.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironmentTests.java:81] This should be added as a new test alongside `getUsernameWhenHasPostgresqlUser`.

We already have tests that verify the handling of `@Autowired` when identifying the constructor to use for value object binding. Here's one example:

https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/blob/2bfd784d7baef889fab89ab406f9fcb762b81c6d/spring-boot-project/spring-boot/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/context/properties/bind/DefaultBindConstructorProviderTests.java#L61-L65

If we want to cover the non-member case as well, I think it should be done in `DefaultBindConstructorProviderTests` rather than here as it's `DefaultBindConstructorProvider` that contains the logic for ignoring private constructors:

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @theodorosidmar. I've left a few comments for your consideration.

- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironment.java:58] I don't think a default is needed here so this could just use `get`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironment.java:59] We don't really use `Objects.equals`. This could be `return "trust".equals(postgresAuthMethod);` instead.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironmentTests.java:82] `getPasswordWhenHasTrustHostAuthMethod()`
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docker-compose/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docker/compose/service/connection/postgres/PostgresEnvironment.java:58] For consistency, I'd rename `postgresAuthMethod` to `hostAuthMethod`.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/liquibase/LiquibaseAutoConfiguration.java:131] This could be `customizer.ifAvailable(liquibase::setCustomizer)`. It would then only be set if the customizer's available which I think is slightly better than setting it to `null`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/liquibase/LiquibaseAutoConfigurationTests.java:539] We prefer longer names for test methods that describe what's being tested. Something like `whenCustomizerBeanIsDefinedThenItIsConfiguredOnSpringLiquibase`.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the latest updates, @onobc.

In addition to my specific comments, I've also spent some time reviewing the configuration properties again. Unfortunately, I'm quite concerned about the sheer number of them. 

Looking at the generated JSON, there are 219 `spring.pulsar` properties. To put that into context, there are 1770 properties in total in `spring-boot-autoconfigure` with 12% of those being for Spring Pulsar. Put another way, adding Spring Pulsar support increases the properties in `spring-boot-autoconfigure` by 14%.

Maybe it's just the nature of the beast (there are 174 `spring.kafka.*` properties) but I do think it would be worth spending some time to see if the configuration model can be simplified. I think that would be of benefit both to us from a maintenance perspective and to users from an ease-of-use perspective.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarAutoConfiguration.java:85] We don't use `var` in Boot's code base. Please declare variables' types explicitly.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarClientBuilderConfigurer.java:148] You can use `MutuallyExclusiveConfigurationPropertiesException` here. Something like this:

```java
MutuallyExclusiveConfigurationPropertiesException.throwIfMultipleNonNullValuesIn((entries) -> {
    entries.put("spring.pulsar.client.auth-params", getAuthParams());
    entries.put("spring.pulsar.client.authentication.*", getAuthentication());
});
```
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarProperties.java:2138] This could be a `MutuallyExclusiveConfigurationPropertiesException` too.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/AuthParameterUtils.java:38] I think this is trying to support relaxed binding for arbitrary properties that have been bound to a `Map<String, String>`. I don't think we should do that here as we don't do it anywhere else. Boot's docs have this warning for `spring.jpa.properties`:

> You need to ensure that names defined under `spring.jpa.properties.*` exactly match those expected by your JPA provider. Spring Boot will not attempt any kind of relaxed binding for these entries.
>
> For example, if you want to configure Hibernate’s batch size you must use `spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.jdbc.batch_size`. If you use other forms, such as `batchSize` or `batch-size`, Hibernate will not apply the setting.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarAutoConfiguration.java:90] This will duplicate the message in the thrown exception and in the cause. Is that necessary? This comment applies to other exceptions added in this PR but not all of them. Things should be consistent and probably not duplicate the message.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarAutoConfiguration.java:159] The return type of a `@Bean` method should be as specific as possible. Could this be `DefaultPulsarConsumerFactory` with `@ConditionalOnMissingBean(PulsarConsumerFactory.class)`, similar to `topicResolver` above?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarAutoConfiguration.java:183] More specific return type? This applies to several other `@Bean` methods as well.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/resources/META-INF/additional-spring-configuration-metadata.json:2079] `org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.pulsar.PulsarReactiveProperties.Sender` has the nested `batch` field first and `cache` last. They should be together. For consistency with existing classes in Boot, I think they should go after all the other property fields.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-dependencies/build.gradle:1114] That's a lot of modules! Could you try to convince them to publish a bom that we could import?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/messaging/pulsar.adoc:12] Is it possible to not expose the imperative components as beans in a reactive app?
- [spring-boot-tests/spring-boot-smoke-tests/spring-boot-smoke-test-pulsar-reactive/src/test/resources/logback-test.xml:12] If we need this in our smoke test, won't users have a bad experience without it? Note that Boot now configures Logback's default logging level to `INFO` even before the app/test has started so Docker Java's logging should be much less verbose than it has been in the past.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarProperties.java:110] Wherever it goes, I'd prefer it to be package-private.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarPropertiesMapper.java:69] Couldn't this use `isTransactionEnabled()` (wherever it ends up)?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarProperties.java:900] "supports" doesn't read quite right to me for a property named `enabled`. Maybe "Whether transactions are enabled" instead?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarProperties.java:908] If someone sets `enabled` to `false` and `required` to `true` what will happen? Judging by the names alone, I think I'd expect a failure as something that's required isn't enabled which feels like misconfiguration to me.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarPropertiesMapper.java:164] Will bad things happen if we accidentally call `customizeClientBuilder` without `customizeTemplate` as transactions would then be partially configured? I'm not sure how it would look, but it feels like it would be good for the code's structure to prevent that mistake. Perhaps a single method for configuration transactions that takes both the template and the client builder?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/pulsar/PulsarPropertiesTests.java:420] I don't feel strongly one way or the other. That said, I do wonder if the intent would be clearer with four separate methods with names that make the expectations clear. They could all then delegate to a common method that looks a lot like this one.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @codefromthecrypt.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/BravePropagationConfigurations.java:108] There's no point in deprecating this as it's package-private API for which the javadoc is not published.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/CompositePropagationFactory.java:76] There's no point in deprecating this as it's package-private API for which the javadoc is not published.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/CompositePropagationFactoryTests.java:148] There's no point in deprecating this as it's test code
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/BravePropagationConfigurations.java:105] My reading of https://github.com/openzipkin/brave/pull/1396 is that Brave 5.18 will require `get` to be implemented. Have I missed something? This change appears to be unrelated to upgrading to Brave 5.17.1.

I'm also not sure about throwing an `UnsupportedOperationException`. Is it not pushing a guaranteed failure to runtime when remove the method would have caught it at compile time instead? If the goal is to provide a smoother migration path, I wonder if `create` could delegate somehow to `get` instead.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/autoconfigure/tracing/LocalBaggageFieldsTests.java:60] There's no point in deprecating this as it's test code

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/context/properties/ConfigurationPropertiesReportEndpoint.java:537] I'm not sure that this is the correct fix, unfortunately. From a nesting perspective, records and classes are the same and the existing comparison of the package names should be sufficient. I suspect that expanding it to whenever a parent is a record may cause problems for richer, non-scalar types that shouldn't be included but now will be.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/context/properties/ConfigurationPropertiesReportEndpointSerializationTests.java:669] I'm a little confused by the naming here. Why is one `exposed` and one `hidden`? As far as I can tell, their visibility in the endpoint's output should be the same and the assertion seems to confirm this.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @JohnNiang. I've left a few comments for your consideration.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/web/ServerProperties.java:335] This should be an `int` with a default value that matches that of `InMemoryWebSessionStore`. Providing a default improves the metadata that's used for auto-completion when editing application properties and YAML files. A test should be added to assert that the defaults are in sync.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/web/reactive/WebFluxAutoConfiguration.java:336] We don't use `var` in Spring Boot's code.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/web/reactive/WebFluxAutoConfiguration.java:337] This `null` check can be removed once the `Integer` is an `int`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/test/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/web/reactive/WebFluxAutoConfigurationTests.java:630] Don't use `var` please.

POINTS:
